constraints.description: Activities are expected to start at TRL 2 and achieve TRL 5 by the end of the project.
This topic implements the co-programmed European Partnership on AI, Data and Robotics (ADRA). All proposals are expected to allocate tasks for cohesion activities with ADRA.
All proposals are expected to allocate tasks for cohesion activities with the CSA HORIZON-CL4-2025-03-HUMAN-18: GenAI4EU central Hub.
Communicable results must be shared with the European R&D community through the AI-on-demand platform.
All proposals are expected to incorporate mechanisms for assessing and demonstrating progress, including qualitative and quantitative KPIs, benchmarking, and progress monitoring.
When possible, proposals should build on and reuse public results from relevant previous funded actions.
Projects selected in this topic must link to the resources offered by the AI Factories, including the Data Labs.
Project results may be validated in the Testing and Experiment Facilities and further deployed via the European Digital Innovation Hubs (EDIHs).
Projects must contribute to the Apply AI Strategy (COM xxxx(2025)) as an explicit implementation objective of this topic.
Proposals should build on or seek collaboration with relevant projects and develop synergies with other relevant international, European, national, or regional initiatives.
Maximum EU contribution per project is EUR 19,000,000 (minimum and maximum both set at EUR 19,000,000).
Submission procedure is single-stage (single deadline model); no two-stage evaluation process applies.

To ensure portfolio coverage, grants will be awarded not only in order of ranking but also to at least one proposal focusing on each of the three sectors: healthcare, advanced manufacturing, and in-vehicle autonomous driving, subject to passing all evaluation thresholds.
Financial support to third parties (FSTP) is provided under this topic: maximum EUR 300,000 per grant at stage 2, EUR 2,250,000 per grant at stage 3, and EUR 2,550,000 per third party.

constraints.assumption_note: Maximum project duration of 48 months is not explicitly stated in the topic text of the call extract or slice. This value is drawn from selected_call.json, which itself carries a note that this is the standard maximum for large RIA actions under Horizon Europe and that it should be verified against General Annex B. Verification against Tier 1 General Annexes is required before using this figure in downstream proposal content.
